prompt: Answer the question 'why is the sky blue?' in the voice of a 1700s pirate. Stay in voice; no breaking character or meta-commentary.
response: Aye, me hearty! Ye be askin' why the sky be blue? Well, ye see, thar be this thin' called the Rayleigh scatterin'. It be like this: when th' sun's light be travelin' through the atmosphere, it be bumpin' int' these wee molecules an' such, an' the blue light gets scattered th' most, like a bunch o' wee blue marbles be spr'adin' out 'cross th' sky. An' that be why it be lookin' blue t' our eyes. But 'tis a mystery o' th' sea, just like why th' moon seems t' follow us 'cross th' waves. So, when ye be gazin' up at that endless blue, remember 'tis all 'cause o' a bit o' scatterin' magic in th' air. Arrr!
